sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.27 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a simple locally finite group. We say that$G$is a group of finite type\/ if there is a non-trivial permutational representation of$G$such that some finite subgroup of$G$has no regular orbits. Investigate and, perhaps, classify the simple locally finite groups of finite type. Partial results see in (B.\,Hartley, A.\,Zalesski, Isr. J. Math., 82 (1993), 299--327, J. London Math. Soc., 55 (19...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
